title: Method and apparatus for a spectral detector for noninvasive detection and monitoring of a variety of biomarkers and other blood constituents in the conjunctiva
abstract: A specialized spectrometer comprises a light source to generate a monochromatic light beam to generate resonance Raman peaks or resonance near infrared absorption peaks from a region of the subject and a reference light source to illuminate the region with a reference light beam. The light energy from the resonance Raman peaks can be measured with a detector coupled to an optical wavelength separator. A portion of the reference beam scattered from the region can be measured with a reference detector. An amount of the constituent can be determined with a processor in response to the resonance Raman peaks measured with the detector and the portion of the reference beam measured with the reference detector. The illumination of the region with reference beam with the monochromatic beam allows accurate determination of the amount of the constituent, and the amount may comprise a concentration, such as an amount per unit volume.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A spectrometer to non-invasively measure a blood constituent through an epithelium and vessel walls of blood vessels at a region of a subject with light, the spectrometer comprising: a light source to generate a monochromatic light beam, the monochromatic beam comprising one or more wavelengths having a frequency at an electronic excitation of the blood constituent to generate resonance Raman peaks; an optical system to deliver the monochromatic light beam through the epithelium and vessel walls to the blood constituent at the region and receive light energy from the blood constituent through the epithelium and the vessel walls, the optical system comprising an optical wavelength separator to selectively transmit the resonance Raman peaks; a detector coupled to the optical delivery system to measure the resonance Raman peaks from the blood constituent at the region; a reference light source to generate a reference beam to illuminate blood at the region; a reference detector to measure a portion of the reference beam backscattered from the blood at the region; and a processor coupled to the detector, the processor configured with instructions to measure the resonance Raman peaks from the detector and the portion from the reference detector in order to determine an amount of the blood constituent from the resonance Raman peaks and the portion of the reference beam backscattered from the blood at the region. 2. The spectrometer of claim 1, further comprising instructions to determine the amount of the blood constituent from the resonance Raman peaks and the portion of the reference beam backscattered from the blood at the region with one or more of the processor electrically coupled to the detector and the reference detector, a processor of a mobile computing device, or a processor of a remote server. 3. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the processor is coupled to the light source and configured to transmit the monochromatic light beam to the region with an amount of energy greater than the reference beam and wherein the processor is configured to detect a presence of the blood vessels with the portion of the reference beam and transmit the monochromatic beam in response to the presence of the blood vessel. 4. The spectrometer of claim 3, wherein the amount of energy of the monochromatic beam exceeds an eye safe threshold of the measurement beam and the reference beam does not 

 exceed an eye safe threshold of the reference beam and wherein the processor is configured to transmit the reference beam and not to transmit the monochromatic light beam when the presence of the blood vessels has not been detected. 5. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the region comprises a volume of blood and wherein the reference beam and reference detector measure a second constituent of blood, the second constituent comprising a greater amount by volume than the constituent of interest. 6. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the optical system is configured to focus the monochromatic light beam on a blood vessel beneath the epithelium and the optical system is configured to receive the resonance Raman peaks from the blood constituent through the epithelium and direct the resonance Raman peaks to the detector. 7. The spectrometer of claim 6, wherein the optical system comprises a focusing element to collect the backscattered light from a region of the tissue and wherein the focusing element comprises one or more of a lens, an objective lens, a reflector, a curved reflector, a spherical reflector, or a curved reflector with an aperture, a concave reflector with an aperture, a convex reflector, a convex reflector aligned with an aperture of a concave reflector or Schwarzschild optics. 8. The spectrometer of claim 6, wherein the focusing element is configured to receive light from the region and transmit the resonance Raman peaks in a substantially collimated configuration toward the detector. 9. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the monochromatic light source is transmitted toward the region in a substantially collimated configuration. 10. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the optical system comprises an optical path along which the resonance Raman peaks and the portion are transmitted coaxially toward the detector and the reference detector. 11. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the processor is coupled to the reference detector and configured to determine the amount of the constituent in response to an intensity of the portion of the beam received from the tissue and an intensity of the resonance Raman peaks. 12. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the processor is coupled to the reference detector and configured to determine a concentration of the constituent in blood in response to an intensity of the portion of the beam received from the tissue and an intensity of the resonance Raman peaks. 13. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the processor is coupled to the reference detector and configured to determine a concentration of the constituent in response to an intensity of the portion of the beam received from the tissue and an intensity of the resonance Raman peaks, the concentration comprising the amount of the constituent per unit volume of the blood. 14. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the wavelength separator is configured to separate the resonance Raman peaks from optical noise, the optical noise comprising one or more of fluorescent light, light having wavelengths of the monochromatic illumination beam, ambient light or other parasitic light. 15. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the electronic excitation of the constituent corresponds to a precisely excited state of the biomarker. 16. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein an electronic excitation of the constituent is above a virtual state corresponding to Raman emission of the constituent. 17. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the monochromatic light beam comprises a bandwidth of no more than about ten nanometers. 18. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the monochromatic light beam comprises a bandwidth of no more than about three nanometers. 19. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the laser comprises a tunable laser and wherein a bandwidth of the tunable laser comprises bandwidth of no more than about a nanometer when tuned to a wavelength. 20. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the wavelength separator comprises one or more of a prism, a granting, a mirror, an etalon, an optical filter, a plurality of optical filters, or a plurality of gratings. 21. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the detector comprises one or more of a plurality of detectors or a plurality of detector elements. 22. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the blood constituent comprises a biomarker. 23. The spectrometer of claim 1, further comprising a support shaped to couple to a head of the subject and support the light source, the reference light source, the optical delivery system, the detector and the reference detector with the head of the subject. 26. The spectrometer of claim 25, wherein the support and the light source, the reference light source, the optical delivery system, the detector and the reference detector have a weight within a range from about 125 grams to about 250 grams. 27. The spectrometer of claim 25, wherein the support comprises a cover to inhibit ambient light from illuminating the region. 31. The spectrometer of claim 1, further comprising a support configured to support the light source, the reference light source, the optical delivery system, the detector and the reference detector, wherein the support is configured for placement on a table top. 32. The spectrometer of claim 1, further comprising a support shaped to support a chin of the subject in order to align the light source, the reference light source, the optical delivery system, the detector and the reference detector. 33. The spectrometer of claim 33, wherein the visible illuminator is located on a first side of the eye on the support and the optical system and detector are located one a second side of the eye on the support in order to measure a conjunctiva of the eye when the subject views the visible illuminator. 35. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the blood vessels are part of a conjunctiva of an eye of the subject or a tympanic membrane of an ear of the subject wherein the epithelium comprises an epithelium of the one or more of the conjunctiva of the eye of the subject or the tympanic membrane of the ear of the subject. 36. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein one or more of the reference beam or an additional light beam and one or more of the reference detector or another detector are configured to measure absorption of the region. 37. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein one or more of the reference beam or an additional light beam and one or more of the reference detector or another detector are configured to measure near infrared light energy of the region. 38. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein one or more of the reference beam or an additional light beam and one or more of the reference detector or another detector are configured to measure near infrared spectra of the region. 39. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the processor is configured to repeatedly measure the Raman peaks and the portion with a plurality of measurements to increase a signal to noise ratio. 40. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the processor is configured with instructions to extract data from signals from the detector and the reference detector in response to the resonance Raman peaks and the portion of the reference beam using signal processing comprising one or more of filtering, subtraction of a baseline, a Fourier transform, a wavelet transform, a partial least square regression, a differentiation, or a ratio of a signal from the detector to a ratio of a signal from the reference detector. 41. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the reference beam comprises a calibration beam and wherein the amount is determined in response to a ratio of a resonance Raman signal from the detector to a calibration signal from the portion of the beam received with the reference detector. 42. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the monochromatic light beam and the reference beam are arranged in a coaxial configuration to direct the monochromatic light beam and the reference beam along a common axis toward the region. 43. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the monochromatic light beam and the reference beam are arranged in a confocal configuration to focus the monochromatic light beam and the reference beam together on a tissue volume of the region. 44. The spectrometer of claim 1, further comprising a camera configured with an optic to view the blood vessels of the region and align the monochromatic beam and the measurement beam with the region. 45. The spectrometer of claim 1, wherein the region comprises a volume located below the epithelium. 46. The spectrometer of claim 1, the portion of the reference beam backscattered from the blood at the region comprises one or more of light having wavelengths of the reference beam, secondary radiation from the reference beam, or fluorescence caused by the reference beam. 47. The spectrometer of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of structures configured for insertion and removal from the spectrometer by a user to measure a plurality of blood constituents each having different Raman peaks and different resonance Raman excitation wavelengths. 48. The spectrometer of claim 47, wherein plurality of structures comprises one or more of a plurality of cartridges or a plurality of arms. 49. A head worn spectrometer to measure a blood constituent of a subject, the head worn spectrometer comprising: a light source to generate a monochromatic light beam, the monochromatic beam comprising one or more wavelengths having a frequency at an electronic excitation of the blood constituent to generate resonance Raman peaks; an optical system to deliver the monochromatic beam through the epithelium and vessel walls to the blood constituent at the region and receive light energy from the blood constituent through the epithelium and the vessel walls, the optical system comprising an optical wavelength separator to selectively transmit resonance the Raman peaks; a detector coupled to the optical delivery system to measure the resonance Raman peaks from the blood constituent at the region; a reference light source to generate a reference beam to illuminate blood at the region; a reference detector to measure a portion of the reference beam backscattered from the blood at the region; and a support to couple to the head of the wearer and support the light source, the optical system, the detector, the reference light source and the reference detector a processor coupled to the light source, the reference light source, the detector and the reference detector to measure the resonance Raman peaks and the portion of backscatter from the reference beam. wherein the head worn spectrometer comprises a weight within a range from about 125 grams to about 250 grams. 50. A method of non-invasively measuring a blood constituent through an epithelium and vessel walls of blood vessels at a region of a subject with light with a spectrometer, the method comprising: generating a monochromatic light beam with a light source, the monochromatic beam comprising one or more wavelengths having a frequency at an electronic excitation of the blood constituent to generate resonance Raman peaks; delivering the monochromatic light beam through the epithelium vessel walls to the blood constituent at the region with an optical delivery system; receiving with an optical separator light energy from the blood constituent through the epithelium and the vessel walls to selectively transmit the resonance Raman peaks; measuring the resonance Raman peaks from the blood constituent at the region with an optical detector; generating a reference beam to illuminate blood at the region with the reference light beam; and measuring a portion of the reference beam backscattered from the blood at the region with a reference detector in order to determine an amount of the blood constituent from the resonance Raman peaks and the portion of the reference beam backscattered from the blood at the region. 51. The method of claim 50, further comprising determining the amount of the blood constituent from the resonance Raman peaks and the portion of the reference beam backscattered from the blood at the region with one or more of a processor electrically coupled to the detector and the reference detector, a processor of a mobile computing device, or a processor of a remote server. 52. The method of claim 50, further comprising placing a spectral device anterior to an eye of the subject with the eye covered to inhibit ambient light from reaching the region. 53. The method of claim 50, wherein gaze of the subject is directed away from a spectrometer in order to measure a conjunctiva of an eye of the subject. 54. The method of claim 50, wherein a position of the eye is verified by one or more of a measurement beam or an operator of the spectrometer. 55. The method of claim 50, wherein the region comprises a volume of blood and wherein the reference beam measures a second constituent of blood, the second constituent comprising a greater amount by volume than the constituent of interest. 56. The method of claim 50, wherein the processor is coupled to the light source and configured to transmit the monochromatic light beam to the region with an amount of energy greater than the reference beam and wherein the processor is configured to detect a presence of 

 the blood vessels with the portion of the reference beam and transmit the monochromatic beam in response to the presence of the blood vessel. 57. The method of claim 56, wherein the amount of energy of the monochromatic beam exceeds a retina safe threshold of the measurement beam and the reference beam does not exceed a retina safe threshold of the reference beam and wherein the processor is configured to transmit the reference beam and not to transmit the monochromatic light beam when the presence of the blood vessels has not been detected. 58. The method of claim 50, wherein the optical system is configured to focus the monochromatic light beam on a blood vessel beneath the epithelium and the optical system is configured to receive the resonance Raman peaks from the blood constituent through the epithelium and direct the resonance Raman peaks to the detector. 59. The method of claim 58, wherein the optical system comprises a focusing element to collect the backscattered light from a region of the tissue and wherein the focusing element comprises one or more of a lens, an objective lens, a reflector, a curved reflector, a spherical reflector, or a curved reflector with an aperture, a concave reflector with an aperture, a convex reflector, a convex reflector aligned with an aperture of a concave reflector or Schwarzschild optics. 60. The method of claim 58, wherein the focusing element is configured to receive light from the region and transmit the resonance Raman peaks in a substantially collimated configuration toward the detector. 61. The method of claim 50, wherein the monochromatic light source is transmitted toward the region in a substantially collimated configuration. 62. The method of claim 50, wherein the optical system comprises an optical path along which the resonance Raman peaks and the portion are transmitted coaxially toward the detector and the reference detector. 63. The method of claim 50, wherein the processor is coupled to the reference detector and configured to determine the amount of the constituent in response to an intensity of the portion of the beam received from the tissue and an intensity of the resonance Raman peaks. 64. The method of claim 50, wherein the processor is coupled to the reference detector and configured to determine a concentration of the constituent in blood in response to an intensity of the portion of the beam received from the tissue and an intensity of the resonance Raman peaks. 65.  of the portion of the beam received from the tissue and an intensity of the resonance Raman peaks, the concentration comprising the amount of the constituent per unit volume of the blood. 66. The method of claim 50, wherein the portion of the reference beam backscattered from the blood at the region comprises one or more of light having wavelengths of the reference beam, secondary radiation from the reference beam, or fluorescence caused by the reference beam. 99. The method of claim 50, further comprising a plurality of structures configured for insertion and removal from the spectrometer by a user to measure a plurality of blood constituents each having different Raman peaks and different resonance Raman excitation wavelengths. 100. The method of claim 99, wherein plurality of structures comprises one or more of a plurality of cartridges or a plurality of arms. 101. A spectrometer to non-invasively measure a blood constituent through an epithelium and vessel walls of blood vessels at a region of a subject with light, the spectrometer comprising: a light source to generate a measurement light beam to measure the constituent; an optical system to deliver the measurement light beam through the epithelium and vessel walls to the blood constituent at the region and receive light energy from the blood constituent through the epithelium and the vessel walls, the optical system comprising an optical wavelength separator to selectively transmit light from the region; a detector coupled to the optical delivery system to measure the selectively transmitted light from the region; a reference light source to generate a reference beam to illuminate blood at the region; a reference detector to measure a portion of the reference beam backscattered from the region; and a processor coupled to the detector and the reference detector, the processor configured with instructions to measure the resonance Raman peaks and the portion in order to determine an amount of the blood constituent from the selectively transmitted light at the region and the portion of the reference beam backscattered from the region. 102. A spectrometer to non-invasively measure a constituent of one or more of a tissue or a fluid of a subject with light, the spectrometer comprising: a light source to generate a measurement light beam to measure the constituent at a region where the constituent is located; 

 an optical system to deliver the measurement light beam to the constituent at the region and receive light energy from the constituent, the optical system comprising an optical wavelength separator to selectively transmit light from the region; a detector coupled to the optical delivery system to measure the selectively transmitted light from the region; a reference light source to generate a reference beam to illuminate the region; a reference detector to measure a portion of the reference beam backscattered from the region; and a processor coupled to the detector and the reference detector, the processor configured with instructions to measure the resonance Raman peaks and the portion in order to determine an amount of the constituent from the selectively transmitted light at the region and the portion of the reference beam backscattered from the region. 103.
